title: Orthochilus trilamellatus
intro: Orthochilus trilamellatus, commonly known as the three-layered orchid or three-layered grass orchid, is a species of orchid endemic to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthochilus trilamellatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Orthochilus trilamellatus is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used to make capsules or tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy:

Orthochilus trilamellatus is classified within the family Orchidaceae. 
More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. 
Further classification places it in the tribe Spiranthinae. 
Finally, it is assigned to the genus Orthochilus.
